response:
# PRODUCT REQUIREMENT DOCUMENT - ANNUAL STATEMENT VIEW AND DOWNLOAD

## CURRENT STATE SUMMARY:
Today, customers can access only the last 24 months of monthly statements within the PIXEL app. For annual or consolidated statements spanning financial years, self-employed users must contact customer support who manually retrieve archived statements and email them to the registered email ID. This creates operational overhead for support teams and delays for customers during tax filing season (typically March-July in India).

---

## SCOPE:

### IN SCOPE:
- View and download annual statements for up to 3 financial years (FY) within the PIXEL app
- Asynchronous PDF generation with 30-minute SLA from request initiation
- Annual statement containing transaction summary, rewards summary, EMI details, add-on card information, and credit limit history
- Request annual statement through customer support agents (phone/chat/email)
- Password-protected PDF for email delivery, non-password-protected for in-app viewing
- Financial year definition: April 1 to March 31 (Indian FY)
- Notification to user when annual statement is ready for download
- Retry mechanism for failed statement generation
- Statement archival and retrieval from storage

### OUT OF SCOPE:
- Calendar year statements (Jan-Dec) — Indian tax filing follows financial year (Apr-Mar) only
- More than 3 financial years of annual statements — data retention policy limits and server load considerations
- Real-time/synchronous statement generation — server load constraints require asynchronous processing
- Customizable date range statements — adds complexity; users can use monthly statements for custom ranges
- Annual statements for accounts less than 3 months old — insufficient data for meaningful annual summary
- Editing or annotating statements within the app — statements are official bank documents requiring immutability
- Sharing statements directly to CA/third-party apps — security and data privacy concerns
- Physical/printed annual statement delivery — only e-statements supported per current SOP

---

## FUNCTIONAL USE CASES:

### Use Case 1: Self-employed user requests annual statement for most recent completed FY
**Actor:** Primary cardholder (self-employed)  
**Precondition:** User has an active PIXEL credit card with at least one completed financial year of transaction history; user is authenticated in the PIXEL app  
**User Story:** As a self-employed credit card user, I want to request and download my FY 2023-24 annual statement from the app so that I can share it with my CA for ITR filing within 5 minutes of accessing the app.

**Steps:**
1. User navigates to Statements section in PIXEL app
2. User selects "Annual Statements" tab
3. System displays available financial years (up to 3 most recent completed FYs)
4. User selects "FY 2023-24" and taps "Request Statement"
5. System validates eligibility and displays confirmation: "Your annual statement is being generated. You'll be notified within 30 minutes when it's ready to download."
6. System queues asynchronous PDF generation job with user_id, FY_year, request_timestamp
7. Background job generates comprehensive PDF with all sections (transactions, rewards, EMI, add-ons, limits)
8. Upon completion (within 30 min), system sends in-app notification and email notification
9. User returns to Annual Statements section, sees "Ready to Download" status
10. User taps "Download" and PDF opens in app viewer (non-password-protected)
11. User taps "Share" icon to export PDF or email to CA

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given user has completed FY 2023-24 (account active from Apr 2023 to Mar 2024), when user navigates to Annual Statements, then system displays "FY 2023-24" as available option with status "Not Requested"
- Given user taps "Request Statement" for FY 2023-24, when request is submitted, then system displays confirmation message with 30-minute SLA and changes status to "Generating"
- Given annual statement generation is complete, when user receives notification, then notification contains deep link to Annual Statements section and statement is downloadable within app
- Given user opens downloaded annual statement in app, when PDF loads, then PDF is not password-protected and contains all sections: cover page, account summary, transaction details, rewards summary, EMI schedule, add-on card details, credit limit history
- Given user has already requested FY 2023-24 statement, when user navigates to Annual Statements again, then system shows "Downloaded on [date]" status with "Download Again" option (no regeneration, retrieves existing PDF)

**Failure State AC:**
- Given statement generation fails due to server error, when 30 minutes elapse, then user receives notification "We couldn't generate your statement. Please try again or contact support" and status shows "Failed - Retry Available"
- Given user requests statement for FY where account was inactive for entire period, when user taps "Request Statement", then system displays error "No transactions found for this financial year" and disables request button
- Given user has poor network connectivity, when downloading PDF, then system displays "Download failed. Check your connection and try again" with retry option

**Priority:** P0

---

### Use Case 2: User requests annual statement for older completed FY (FY-2 or FY-3)
**Actor:** Primary cardholder  
**Precondition:** User has active card with 2+ completed financial years of history; user is authenticated in app  
**User Story:** As a credit card user filing revised returns, I want to download my FY 2021-22 annual statement so that I can provide historical financial records to tax authorities.

**Steps:**
1. User navigates to Statements > Annual Statements
2. System displays list of available FYs: "FY 2023-24", "FY 2022-23", "FY 2021-22" (up to 3 most recent completed)
3. User selects "FY 2021-22" and taps "Request Statement"
4. System checks if statement was previously generated and stored
5. If exists: System immediately shows "Ready to Download" and allows download (no regeneration)
6. If not exists: System queues generation job, displays 30-min SLA message
7. User receives notification when ready
8. User downloads and views PDF

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given user's account is 4 years old (opened FY 2020-21), when user views Annual Statements, then system displays only 3 most recent completed FYs: "FY 2023-24", "FY 2022-23", "FY 2021-22" (FY 2020-21 not shown)
- Given user previously requested FY 2021-22 statement 6 months ago, when user selects FY 2021-22 again, then system retrieves existing PDF from archive within 5 seconds without regeneration
- Given user requests FY 2021-22 for first time, when generation completes, then PDF contains accurate historical data including closed EMIs, expired rewards, and deactivated add-on cards from that period
- Given current date is May 15, 2024, when user views available FYs, then system shows completed FYs only: "FY 2023-24" (Apr 2023-Mar 2024), "FY 2022-23", "FY 2021-22" but not "FY 2024-25" (current incomplete FY)

**Failure State AC:**
- Given archived statement for FY 2021-22 is corrupted or missing from storage, when user requests download, then system displays "Statement unavailable. Regenerating now - you'll be notified in 30 minutes" and queues new generation job
- Given user account was closed and reopened, when user requests statement for FY during closure period, then system displays "Account was inactive during this period. No statement available."

**Priority:** P0

---

### Use Case 3: Customer support agent requests annual statement on behalf of user
**Actor:** HDFC customer support agent  
**Precondition:** Agent is authenticated in support center/ops center; customer has called/emailed requesting annual statement; customer identity is verified  
**User Story:** As a customer support agent, I want to generate and email annual statements to verified customers so that I can resolve their requests within one call without asking them to use the app.

**Steps:**
1. Agent receives customer request for annual statement (phone/email/chat)
2. Agent verifies customer identity using existing verification protocol
3. Agent logs into Support Center and searches customer by card number/mobile/CIF
4. Agent navigates to customer's Statement Management section
5. Agent selects "Generate Annual Statement" option
6. Agent selects financial year from dropdown (up to 3 available FYs)
7. Agent confirms customer's registered email address
8. System queues asynchronous generation job
9. System displays confirmation: "Annual statement for [FY] will be emailed to [email] within 30 minutes. Ticket ID: [auto-generated]"
10. Agent communicates SLA to customer and provides ticket ID
11. When generation completes, system sends password-protected PDF to customer's registered email
12. System logs action in customer's activity history with agent ID, timestamp, FY requested

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given agent searches for customer in Support Center, when customer record loads, then Annual Statement section displays available FYs and previous request history (date, FY, status, requested by)
- Given agent initiates annual statement generation, when request is submitted, then system auto-generates ticket ID, logs agent ID, customer ID, FY, timestamp, and email address in audit trail
- Given statement generation completes, when PDF is emailed to customer, then email contains password-protected PDF with password format: [Last 4 digits of card][DDMMYY of DOB] (e.g., 1234010190 for card ending 1234, DOB Jan 1, 1990)
- Given agent requests statement for FY already generated within last 7 days, when agent submits request, then system displays warning "Statement for this FY was generated on [date]. Regenerate or resend existing?" with two options
- Given customer's email ID is invalid/bounced previously, when agent attempts to send statement, then system displays error "Email delivery failed previously. Update email address before proceeding" with link to update email

**Failure State AC:**
- Given statement generation fails after 30 minutes, when agent checks ticket status, then system shows "Generation Failed - Retry Required" with error code and option to retry or escalate to L2 support
- Given customer requests statement for FY where account had fraud/dispute flags, when agent attempts generation, then system displays "Unable to generate - Account flagged. Escalate to [specific team]" and blocks generation

**Priority:** P0

---

### Use Case 4: System generates annual statement PDF asynchronously with all required sections
**Actor:** Backend system (async job processor)  
**Precondition:** User or agent has initiated annual statement request; request is queued in job processor; all transaction, rewards, EMI, and account data is available in database  
**User Story:** As the PIXEL backend system, I want to generate comprehensive annual statement PDFs asynchronously so that I can handle multiple concurrent requests without impacting app performance while ensuring data accuracy.

**Steps:**
1. Job processor picks queued request from generation queue (FIFO)
2. System retrieves user_id, card_number, FY_start_date (Apr 1), FY_end_date (Mar 31)
3. System queries transaction database for all transactions between FY dates
4. System calculates transaction summary: total spends, total payments, total refunds, category-wise breakdown
5. System queries rewards database for points earned, redeemed, expired during FY
6. System queries EMI database for all EMIs active during FY (principal, interest, tenure, status)
7. System retrieves add-on card details active during FY (card numbers, activation dates, user names)
8. System retrieves credit limit history (limit changes, temporary limit increases during FY)
9. System retrieves account summary: opening balance (Apr 1), closing balance (Mar 31), total amount due, minimum due for each month
10. System compiles data into PDF template with sections: Cover page, Account Summary, Transaction Details (month-wise), Rewards Summary, EMI Schedule, Add-on Cards, Credit Limit History, Important Notes
11. System generates PDF, stores in secure storage with naming convention: [card_number]_AnnualStatement_FY[YYYY-YY].pdf
12. System updates request status to "Completed" with storage_path, generation_timestamp
13. System triggers notification service to send in-app and email notifications
14. System logs generation metrics: time_taken, file_size, sections_included

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given statement generation job is queued, when job processor picks request, then generation completes within 30 minutes for 95% of requests and within 45 minutes for 99% of requests
- Given FY 2023-24 statement is being generated, when system queries transactions, then all transactions from Apr 1, 2023 00:00:00 to Mar 31, 2024 23:59:59 are included with no duplicates or omissions
- Given user had 3 EMIs active during FY (1 completed, 1 ongoing, 1 foreclosed), when EMI section is generated, then PDF shows all 3 EMIs with accurate principal, interest, tenure, and status for each
- Given user earned 15,000 reward points, redeemed 5,000, and 2,000 expired during FY, when rewards summary is generated, then PDF shows opening balance, earned, redeemed, expired, and closing balance with transaction-level details
- Given user had 2 add-on cards (1 active, 1 blocked mid-year), when add-on section is generated, then PDF shows both cards with activation dates, user names, and status changes during FY
- Given user's credit limit increased from ₹2L to ₹3L in Aug 2023, when credit limit history is generated, then PDF shows timeline: Apr 2023: ₹2L, Aug 2023: ₹3L with effective dates
- Given PDF generation completes, when file is stored, then file size is optimized (max 5MB) and stored in encrypted format with access controls limiting retrieval to user_id and authorized agents only

**Failure State AC:**
- Given transaction data query times out after 2 minutes, when system retries 3 times unsuccessfully, then system marks job as "Failed - Data Retrieval Error", logs error code, and triggers alert to ops team
- Given PDF generation fails due to template rendering error, when system detects failure, then system logs error details, marks status as "Failed - Generation Error", and queues for manual review by ops team
- Given storage service is unavailable, when system attempts to save PDF, then system retries 5 times with exponential backoff, and if still failing, marks job as "Failed - Storage Error" and alerts ops team

**Priority:** P0

---

### Use Case 5: User receives notification when annual statement is ready and downloads from app
**Actor:** Primary cardholder  
**Precondition:** User has requested annual statement; PDF generation is complete; user has PIXEL app installed with notifications enabled  
**User Story:** As a credit card user who requested an annual statement, I want to receive a notification when it's ready so that I can download it immediately without repeatedly checking the app.

**Steps:**
1. System completes annual statement PDF generation
2. System triggers notification service with user_id, FY, statement_ready_timestamp
3. Notification service sends in-app push notification: "Your FY 2023-24 Annual Statement is ready to download"
4. Notification service sends email to registered email: "Your PIXEL Annual Statement is Ready" with deep link to app
5. User taps push notification or email link
6. App opens directly to Annual Statements section
7. User sees FY 2023-24 with status "Ready to Download" and download icon
8. User taps "Download" button
9. System retrieves PDF from secure storage
10. PDF opens in in-app viewer (non-password-protected)
11. User can scroll through all sections, zoom, and use share functionality
12. User taps "Share" icon to export via email, WhatsApp, or save to device

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given annual statement generation completes at 10:15 AM, when notification is triggered, then user receives push notification within 2 minutes and email notification within 5 minutes
- Given user taps push notification, when app opens, then app navigates directly to Annual Statements section with requested FY highlighted and "Download" button visible
- Given user taps "Download" button, when PDF loads in app viewer, then PDF opens within 5 seconds and is not password-protected (user already authenticated in app)
- Given user views PDF in app, when user scrolls through document, then all sections are rendered correctly: cover page, account summary, transactions (month-wise tables), rewards summary, EMI details, add-on cards, credit limit history, footer with page numbers
